历年位次与招生计划.title: Historical Rankings and Enrollment Plans
历年位次与招生计划.text: From 2021 to 2025, the minimum rankings for science (physics category) were approximately 21,866, 43,252, 24,444, 27,919, and 31,705 respectively. In 2022, due to a significant drop in the provincial science first-tier line, an abnormally high ranking occurred. Excluding this anomaly, the rankings in recent three years show a backward trend, with the 2025 baseline at about 31,705. The university's enrollment plan in Henan for science (physics category) was 208 in 2021, 210 in 2022, 204 in 2023, 208 in 2024, and 199 in 2025. In 2026, the plan increases to 250, an expansion of about 25.6% compared to 2025, which is significant and eases competition pressure.

学校层次与声誉.title: University Level and Reputation
学校层次与声誉.text: It is a 211 Project and Double First-Class construction university. In the 2026 Soft Science China University Main Ranking, it is approximately 56th, with recent rankings fluctuating between 48th and 61st, indicating a stable overall level. The university's reputation has been stable in recent years, with no significant negative public opinion or record of admission gaps. Its Douyin platform attention is average, not entering the high-heat list, and there are no abnormal fluctuations in application popularity. The university has traditional advantages in geology and geological resources and geological engineering, which attract specific groups of candidates.

专业热度与地域.title: Major Popularity and Region
专业热度与地域.text: Engineering majors such as computer science, electronic information/communication, and electrical engineering continue to have high social popularity, positively influencing science candidates' willingness to apply. In 2026, the total number of college entrance exam registrants in Henan Province is about 1.2 million, a significant increase of about 32% compared to 2025 (approximately 907,000), intensifying competition.

预测结论.title: Prediction Conclusion
预测结论.text: Considering the 2025 baseline ranking of 31,705, the significant enrollment expansion of about 25.6% in 2026 (making it harder to enter), the substantial increase of about 32% in the number of local candidates further intensifying competition, and the stable university level and reputation, along with the additional competitive pressure from the surge in candidate numbers, the predicted minimum ranking is about 36,780, with a range of 36,280 to 37,280.

If your ranking is near the upper limit (around 37,280), prioritize Tier 3 majors as safety, while also considering Tier 2 moderate majors.
